era: 1980-1988. Glam Punk/Grunge. E=7 -- theatrical, heavy, the glam-punk synthesis. Bainbridge Island. Independent.
associated_musicians: Mother Love Bone (Andrew Wood with Gossard + Ament)
Temple of the Dog (Chris Cornell tribute to Andrew Wood)

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST BAINBRIDGE ISLAND entry in engine at degree 295. The island across the ferry from Seattle. Andrew Wood grew up there -- island isolation + access to Bowie/Bolan records + ferry connection to Seattle scene = the glam punk/grunge synthesis that no one else in the city was developing.
theory_nodes.description: Bainbridge Island enters the engine at 295.

theory_nodes.concept: Andrew Wood -- L'Andrew the Love God. Power-rock tenor with theatrical commitment. Bowie, Bolan, Plant as primary influences. In the flannel-and-denim scene, performed in tights, scarves, gold lame. Not irony: genuine commitment to theatricality as artistic principle.
theory_nodes.description: Andrew Wood: the voice, the persona, the synthesis.

theory_nodes.concept: Deep Six (1986): Malfunkshun alongside Green River, Soundgarden, Melvins, Skin Yard, U-Men. Jack Endino at Reciprocal. Through Deep Six, Wood met Gossard + Ament. The connection that produced Mother Love Bone.
theory_nodes.description: Deep Six: Malfunkshun at the founding document.

theory_nodes.concept: Glam punk/grunge synthesis: Wood's primary influences (Bowie, Bolan, Plant) + proto-grunge guitar heaviness = something no other Seattle band was doing. This synthesis, scaled up with Gossard/Ament in Mother Love Bone, produced the most ambitious Seattle record of the pre-Nevermind era.
theory_nodes.description: The synthesis Andrew Wood invented on Bainbridge Island.

cross_references.relationship: Deep Six companion. Gossard + Ament left Green River to join Wood in Mother Love Bone. 294 and 295 are consecutive because they are consecutive steps in the same origin story.
Malfunkshun is the origin; Mother Love Bone is the convergence. Wood's glam punk synthesis + Gossard/Ament's proto-grunge architecture. The synthesis at full scale.
Cornell's tribute to Andrew Wood. Malfunkshun is the first chapter. ToD is the memorial. Pearl Jam is the aftermath.
Bruce Fairweather, who played in Malfunkshun, became a founding member of Love Battery. The Malfunkshun-Love Battery personnel connection.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 (Malfunkshun) + E=6 (Rhinoceros Auklet) = E=13. Bainbridge Island band pairs with island alcid colony. Both island-derived, both carrying full identity into the night.
